questions.prompt: A function f(x) represents the distance (in kilometers) traveled by a car at time x (in hours). If f(2) = 85 and f(5) = 190, what is the average rate of change of the car's distance over the interval [2, 5]?
questions.explanation: The average rate of change over an interval [a, b] is calculated using the formula: (f(b) - f(a)) / (b - a). Here, we compute (f(5) - f(2)) / (5 - 2) = (190 - 85) / 3 = 105 / 3 = 35 km/hour. This represents the constant speed the car would need to travel to cover the same distance in the same time period.
